QF.id: I Clearing Settlement and Risk Management_9

question.text: For calendar spread positions in currency futures, the extreme loss margin is levied on:

answer.text: One-third of the mark-to-market value of the open position of the far month contract
The full mark-to-market value of both legs
No ELM is charged on calendar spreads

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Calendar spread ELM = ELM on 1/3 of far month position's MTM value. This reduced margin reflects the lower risk of spread positions compared to outright positions. **The Trap:** It's 1/3 of the FAR month (not near month) and of MTM value (not notional). **Cross-Exam Context:** Calendar spread margin is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Calendar spreads have lower margin requirements because the two legs partially offset each other's risk.
